Here's a description of what's visible in the screenshot:

The screen shows an instructional coding video interface, likely related to p5.js or a similar JavaScript graphics library. The main application visible is Miro, an online collaborative whiteboard tool, with the board titled "Cyclical Functions".  A code editor is open in a separate tab (labeled "p5.js Web Editor | adding pair..."), but the code itself is not visible in this screenshot. The Miro board is currently blank except for the title text, and a sidebar on the left provides drawing tools (pen, shapes, etc.). The bottom right corner shows zoom controls at 19%.

Here's a description of what's visible in the screenshot:

The screen shows an instructional coding environment, likely a web-based IDE. The main application is Miro, with a board titled "functions - Miro" open.  A large blank canvas dominates the central area, with a green heading "Cyclical Functions" at the top. 

The left sidebar of Miro contains various tools for drawing and adding elements (text, shapes, sticky notes). A code editor window titled "p5.js Web Editor | adding pair..." is visible in a separate tab, but its contents are currently empty. The bottom right corner displays zoom controls (19%) and a plus/minus button.

```
// p5.js Web Editor | adding pair...
```

The browser tabs at the top indicate related resources like MSU NetID, Gmail, Canvas, and links to computing labs.

Here's a description of what's visible in the screenshot:

The screen shows an instructional coding video interface, likely related to p5.js or a similar JavaScript graphics library. The main application is Miro, with a board titled "functions." A canvas displays a dark green sine wave curve with the text “SIN(°)” below it. 

The left sidebar of Miro contains drawing tools (pen, arrow, shape tools). A code editor window is open in a separate tab titled "p5.js Web Editor | functions.js," but the code itself is not fully visible; only a small portion at the top reads:

```javascript
// p5.js Web Editor | functions.js
```

Other tabs visible include "QIT," "MSU NetID," "Gmail," and links to resources like "Canvas," "NEST," "T Computing Labs," etc. The bottom toolbar shows zoom controls (19%) and a presentation button.

Here's a description of what's visible in the screenshot:

The screen shows an instructional coding video interface, likely related to p5.js or a similar JavaScript graphics library. The main application is Miro, with a board titled "functions - Miro".  A canvas displays a hand-drawn cyclical function resembling the sine wave `sin(x)`, with key points labeled as 0, π, and 2π along the x-axis, and -1 on the y-axis. A red "X" marks a point on the curve. 

The left sidebar of Miro contains drawing tools (pen, sticky notes, shapes). A code editor tab labeled "p5.js Web Editor | functions : mi..." is open in a separate browser tab, but the code within it is not fully visible. The editor appears to contain some initial setup or drawing commands, but the text is too small and blurry to transcribe accurately. The Miro board also has links to "MSU NetID", "Google Canvas", and other resources.

Here's a description of what's visible in the screenshot:

The screen shows a Miro board titled "functions - Miro". The main content is a hand-drawn diagram illustrating the cyclical function *sin(x)*.  The sine wave is sketched in red, with key points labeled: 0, π/2, π, 3π/2, and 2π along the x-axis. A red 'X' marks a point on the curve, and an angle θ is also indicated. 

A sidebar on the left provides drawing tools (pen, sticky notes, text, shapes).  The top bar shows tabs for "Canvas", "Frames", and other Miro features.

```
p5.js Web Editor | adding pair ...
```
